entities.description: A digital edition-level selection containing the 1921 revised title page and numbered texts I-XIII on printed pp. 1-48. It omits the prefatory note, Notes pp. 49-50, Glossary and Index pp. 51-55, the companion Officium brochure, advertisements, and other bound matter.
entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00058

rights.basis: The item page states that the materials are in the public domain and gives the DPLA No Copyright - United States rights statement; the collection page explicitly qualifies the public-domain assertion to the United States.
rights.component: Materials presented in U-M item CME00058, including the electronic transcription and interface text

bibliographic.citation: George G. Perry, ed., English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole, revised text by Frederick J. Furnivall, glossary revised and notes added by Mabel Day (London: published for the Early English Text Society by Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1921), EETS Original Series 20.
bibliographic.pages: Prefatory note; text pp. 1-48; Notes pp. 49-50; Glossary and Index pp. 51-55
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 20
bibliographic.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole

entities.description: The immediate print source for CME00058: a 1921 reissue of EETS Original Series 20 with a revised text prepared by Frederick J. Furnivall, a glossary revised by Mabel Day, and notes added by Day. The selection prints thirteen numbered items on pp. 1-48.
entities.label: Perry and Furnivall, English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole, revised reissue (1921)
notes: The title page retains the dates '1866, 1921'; the prefatory note calls this a reissue and identifies the revised-text, glossary, and notes responsibilities.
CME00058 encodes the title page and the complete revised text pp. 1-48, but none of the prefatory note, Notes, Glossary and Index, advertisements, or companion brochure.

rights.basis: The revised reissue was published in 1921; current U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the public domain in the United States.
rights.component: The 1921 revised text, prefatory note, Notes, revised glossary, and other edition-level contribution
notes: This U.S. status does not by itself authorize reuse of separately protected provider photography or override access contracts outside the United States.

bibliographic.citation: George G. Perry, ed., English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole: Who Died A.D. 1349 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co., 1866), EETS Original Series 20.
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co.
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 20
bibliographic.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole: Who Died A.D. 1349

entities.description: Perry's first edition and the prior version of the 1921 text. It has ten numbered items: the two contrition narratives are one item, the enclosed-maiden exemplum and Marian prayers are absent, and the later items therefore have different numbers.
entities.label: George G. Perry, English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1866)
notes: The text occupies pp. 1-45 and the glossary pp. 46-49.
Its scope corresponds to revised items I-V and VII-XII, with revised III-IV combined as one 1866 item. Revised items VI and XIII do not occur.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1866; current U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the public domain in the United States.
rights.component: Perry's 1866 edition, preface, transcription, annotations, and glossary

Map the CME00058 portion separately as ff. 192r-196v, 219v-221v, and 223r-230v, with the skipped intervening matter retained in the supplied object.

rights.basis: The library offers charged digital images on request, but no item-level terms for a not-yet-supplied complete object were available.
rights.component: Prospective Lincoln Cathedral-supplied complete-object photography

entities.description: Robert Thornton's c. 1430-1450 paper miscellany and the principal manuscript source for Perry's selection. The selected texts occupy discontinuous loci from ff. 192r-230v amid extensive adjacent devotional, literary, and medical material.

holding.notes: Relevant loci are ff. 192r-196v, 219v-221v, 223r-230v, with substantial non-selected matter between them.

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91

physicalDescription.extent: 322 numbered leaves, plus six paper leaves at front and three at back, from an original minimum of 340
physicalDescription.format: Composite literary, devotional, and medical codex in Robert Thornton's hand; current 1975 S. R. Cockerell binding
notes: Current cataloging records damaged or missing matter and several fragments.
The selected Rolle/Hilton material is only a small, discontinuous portion of the whole codex.

rights.basis: The component texts and surviving manuscript copy are medieval, and their selected readings were published in 1866 or 1921; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.
rights.component: The medieval textual content selected from ff. 192r-230v
notes: This status does not determine rights or contractual conditions in modern manuscript photography, catalog text, or supplied digital files.

rights.basis: The library offers charged digital images on request, but no item-level terms for a not-yet-supplied complete object were available.
rights.component: Prospective Lincoln Cathedral-supplied complete-object photography

access.contact: permissions@bl.uk for permission to copy or reproduce supplied material; licensing@bl.uk for publication enquiries
access.entityLabel: British Library Harley MS 1022
access.format: Reference or reproduction photography; manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned
notes: Request the entire physical object, including covers or boards, spine, five modern front and four rear flyleaves, the paper pastedown, stub, ff. 15* and 16*, blanks, every numbered leaf, and all adjacent works; request color explicitly.
Identify the target portion separately as f. 61v prayers and ff. 62r-64r Oleum effusum.
The current price list is per page/image and a whole-object order is likely to require an individual quote and substantial cost.

rights.basis: The January 2026 Imaging Services terms state that supplied material may carry intellectual-property rights and must not be copied or reproduced without British Library written permission.
rights.component: Prospective British Library-supplied complete-object photography of Harley MS 1022
notes: A paid reference copy may be cached privately only under the supplied terms; repository publication, redistribution, and print-on-demand use require separately documented permission.

entities.description: A Northern English religious miscellany of c. 1390-c. 1425. Perry collated its Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r and the 1921 revision prints the two Marian prayers from f. 61v.

holding.notes: The prayers are at f. 61v and Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1022

physicalDescription.extent: ff. 97 + 15* + 16*, plus five unfoliated modern front flyleaves, four rear flyleaves, a paper stub, and a pastedown
physicalDescription.format: Religious prose-and-verse miscellany in a post-1600 British Museum binding
notes: The British Library catalog localizes the manuscript to Northern England.
A complete-object surrogate must retain every flyleaf, pastedown, stub, blank, numbered leaf, cover or board, spine, and adjacent work.

rights.basis: The component texts and manuscript copy are medieval and the selected readings were published no later than 1921; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.
rights.component: The medieval Oleum effusum and Marian-prayer texts at ff. 61v-64r
notes: Modern photographs and supplied-image terms are separate layers.

rights.basis: The January 2026 Imaging Services terms state that supplied material may carry intellectual-property rights and must not be copied or reproduced without British Library written permission.

entities.description: A fifteenth-century vellum theological miscellany. Its incomplete copy of Walter Hilton's Epistle on the Mixed Life begins at f. 57 and supplies the opening of Perry's item XI through printed p. 27 line 29, after which Perry changes to the incomplete Lincoln witness.

holding.notes: The Mixed Life begins at f. 57 and the following cataloged item begins at f. 65, bounding the component at approximately ff. 57r-64v. Perry does not identify the exact folio at which his supplied opening ends.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 C XVIII

notes: The current catalog requires a letter of introduction for consultation.
A complete-object surrogate must retain every exterior and interior surface and all adjacent treatises, not only ff. 57r-64v.

rights.basis: The component text and manuscript copy are medieval and the selected reading was published in 1866; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.
rights.component: The fifteenth-century copy of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life at approximately ff. 57r-64v

rights.basis: The January 2026 Imaging Services terms state that supplied material may carry intellectual-property rights and must not be copied or reproduced without British Library written permission.

access.entityLabel: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.5.64
access.format: Professional color photography or an available surrogate as approved
notes: Ask first for confirmation of the complete physical extent and exact loci cited in the 1921 Notes.

For whole-object evidence, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf, and all adjacent works; state the target passages separately rather than ordering only those passages.
Request publication or repository-use permission separately from image supply if reuse is contemplated.

rights.component: Prospective Cambridge University Library-supplied complete-object photography
rights.jurisdiction: Cambridge University Library supply and licensing terms

entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century manuscript of works associated with Richard Rolle. Mabel Day's 1921 Notes explicitly cite Dd. v. 64 for readings on printed p. 13 and use 'MS. Camb.' repeatedly on pp. 17-20. Whether every generic Cambridge citation refers to Dd.5.64 requires confirmation.

holding.notes: The exact medieval-manuscript catalog entry was not available as a current item-level web record; CUL states that most medieval manuscript descriptions remain in its printed catalogs.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.5.64

rights.basis: The manuscript and cited textual readings are medieval and were published in sources before 1931.
rights.component: Medieval textual readings cited by the 1921 Notes

rights.basis: CUL distinguishes private-study image supply from academic or commercial use and requires a use-specific imaging/licensing request for publication or other reuse.

access.entityLabel: Unidentified Cambridge University Library witness of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life

entities.description: The Cambridge witness compared in Perry's item XI headnote. Perry says that it is in a later dialect than both the Lincoln and Royal copies and prints one comparative sentence, but supplies no shelfmark. It is not silently equated with Dd.5.64.

holding.notes: Perry's 1866 and 1921 text gives only 'Cambridge University Library.'

entities.label: Unidentified Cambridge University Library witness of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life

next_steps: Request a complete-object quotation rather than a CME00058 leaf excerpt.
After receipt, map only ff. 192r-196v, 219v-221v, and 223r-230v as the work_portion while retaining all intervening and surrounding matter.
Record supplied-file color, resolution, completeness, and use terms before caching or reuse.
openQuestions.question: Can Lincoln Cathedral supply a complete digital MS 91 object including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all front and rear leaves, blanks, fragments, every numbered leaf, edges if offered, and all adjacent works, and under what private-use and redistribution terms?

next_steps: Submit separate whole-object enquiries naming every exterior and interior surface, blank, flyleaf, pastedown, stub, numbered leaf, and adjacent work.
For Harley identify f. 61v and ff. 62r-64r separately; for Royal identify approximately ff. 57r-64v and ask for the precise Perry-opening crosswalk.
Request color explicitly, obtain an itemized quote, and seek written permission before any repository redistribution or print-on-demand use.
openQuestions.question: Will the British Library approve and quote complete-object color photography of Harley MS 1022 and Royal MS 17 C XVIII, and what exact research-cache, repository, and publication permissions would attach to the supplied files?

next_steps: Ask CUL Special Collections to identify the comparison sentence printed in Perry's item XI headnote.
Check the printed Dd.5.64 catalog description and manuscript contents against Day's p. 13 and pp. 17-20 variants.
Keep the unidentified Mixed Life witness distinct until the shelfmark is evidenced.
openQuestions.question: What is the shelfmark of Perry's unnamed Cambridge Mixed Life witness, and can folio-level inspection confirm the high-confidence inference that Mabel Day's contiguous abbreviated 'MS. Camb.' citations refer to CUL Dd.5.64?

next_steps: Compare the 1866 and 1921 texts mechanically and classify substantive versus typographic changes.
Search EETS editorial correspondence and Furnivall papers for the revision files.
Do not treat Day's later Notes citations as proof that every cited witness directly supplied the revised text.
openQuestions.question: Which witnesses and editorial interventions directly produced Furnivall's reset 1921 text beyond the demonstrable added Lincoln and Harley items?

next_steps: Consult modern critical editions and current authorial canons for each individual component.
Retain manuscript rubrics, Perry's historical attributions, and current catalog attributions as separately sourced assertions.
Do not propagate the edition title's single-author wording to the complete selection.
openQuestions.question: How should modern scholarship delimit Rolle's authorship among revised items I-IX and Walter Hilton's or anonymous authorship among items X-XIII?

next_steps: Report the internal wrapper, title-page imprint, ten-item numbering, and text/glossary pagination evidence to BSB/MDZ.
Retain the provider's current metadata verbatim in the cached manifest while cataloging the image content as 1866.
openQuestions.question: Will BSB correct the 1921 creation date and associated bibliographic metadata on bsb10748408 to identify the photographed 1866 first edition?

reviewNotes: This record treats the 1921 revised edited selection, its 1866 prior version, the component manuscripts, scans, U-M encoding, and repository XML as distinct layers.

The title-page attribution to Richard Rolle is historical edition framing, not a reliable authorship statement for every component. Current cataloging identifies Walter Hilton's Of Angels' Song and Epistle on the Mixed Life, while the final prayers are anonymous.

A complete cached facsimile means the full provider-exposed physical book or manuscript object was retained, including covers, blanks, adjacent works, and later matter when exposed. The corpus work is mapped inside that object only through local_copies[].work_portion.

The BSB IIIF metadata dates its object to 1921, but direct inspection of its internal wrapper, title page, preface, ten-item numbering, and imprint establishes that the imaged edition is Perry's 1866 first edition. Both assertions are preserved.
Perry's 1921 headnote prints Harl. MS 1002 for the Marian prayers; the British Library catalog and DIMEV identify the witness as Harley MS 1022, fol. 61v.

lineage.summary: CME00058 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic selection from the 1921 revised EETS reissue of George G. Perry's English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole. The encoding retains the revised title page and all thirteen numbered texts on printed pp. 1-48, but omits the prefatory note, Mabel Day's notes and revised glossary, advertisements, and the separately distributed Officium brochure. The 1921 text is a materially changed version of Perry's 1866 first edition: it splits the two contrition narratives, adds the enclosed-maiden exemplum and two Marian prayers, and renumbers the collection. Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 supplies the principal, discontinuous manuscript portions; Royal MS 17 C XVIII supplies the lost opening of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life; Harley MS 1022 supplies variants for Oleum effusum and the two Marian prayers. Complete scans of two 1921 bound copies and two 1866 copies are cached. No publicly obtainable whole-object facsimile was verified for Lincoln MS 91, Harley MS 1022, Royal MS 17 C XVIII, or Cambridge Dd.5.64, so complete-object reproduction routes and exact work loci are recorded separately.

catalogSummary.author: Richard Rolle (attributed), Walter Hilton, and anonymous or uncertain authors
catalogSummary.date: Fourteenth-century works in late-fourteenth- and fifteenth-century witnesses; first edition 1866, revised thirteen-item reissue 1921
catalogSummary.region: England; principally Northern English witnesses, with a Midland opening supplied for the Mixed Life
catalogSummary.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole

catalogingSubject.label: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The thirteen-part medieval devotional selection as revised and published in EETS Original Series 20 in 1921 and represented by CME00058. This is not one medieval work by one author, not Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 as a whole, not Perry's materially different ten-item 1866 first edition, not the complete 1921 printed volume with notes and glossary, and not the University of Michigan encoding or repository XML as digital objects.

contentParts.label: I. Of the Virtues of the Holy Name of Jesus
notes: Perry uses Lincoln as text and prints Harley variants in footnotes.
contentParts.summary: A devotional exposition of the name Jesus as salvation, sweetness, love, healing, and contemplative joy.

contentParts.label: II. A Tale of Hampole's Temptation
contentParts.summary: A first-person exemplum in which a female-appearing devil tempts the hermit and is defeated through prayer and the sign of the cross; the narrative closes with a two-line prayer.

contentParts.label: III. On Imperfect Contrition
notes: This and item IV were one numbered item III in the 1866 first edition.
contentParts.summary: An exemplum, attributed in the manuscript to Richard the Hermit retelling Caesarius, about a Paris canon whose confession and promised amendment lack true contrition.

contentParts.label: IV. A Story of Perfect Contrition
notes: The 1921 revision promotes this second narrative to a new item IV; it was continuous with item III in 1866.
contentParts.summary: A companion exemplum about a penitent scholar whose written sins are miraculously erased before he can speak them, signifying forgiveness through true contrition.

contentParts.label: V. The Bee and the Stork
contentParts.summary: A moral allegory using the industrious, grounded, clean-winged bee and the heavy stork to teach labor, humility, charity, and freedom from earthly attachment.

contentParts.label: VI. Of the Life of a Certain Enclosed Maiden for the Love of Christ
notes: This numbered item is new in the 1921 revised text and is absent from Perry's 1866 edition.
contentParts.summary: An exemplum, said to be retold from Heraclides by Richard the Hermit, about a maiden who lives enclosed in a sepulchre to avoid occasioning sin and devotes herself to prayer, work, and contemplation.

contentParts.label: VII. A Notable Treatise on the Ten Commandments
contentParts.summary: A concise vernacular exposition of the Decalogue addressing idolatry, witchcraft, oaths, Sabbath observance, obedience, violence, sexual conduct, theft, false witness, and covetousness.

contentParts.label: VIII. Of the Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost
contentParts.summary: A short didactic account of counsel, understanding, wisdom, strength, piety, knowledge, and fear of God as guides to righteous and contemplative life.

contentParts.label: IX. Of Delight and Yearning for God
contentParts.summary: A mystical devotional tract on pure and stable delight in Jesus, restraint of sensual desire, disciplined thought, and spiritual rest in divine love.

contentParts.label: X. Of Angels' Song
notes: Perry's supplied heading is The Anehede of Godd with mannis saule and his edition historically ascribes the untitled manuscript text to Rolle; current specialist cataloging identifies it as Walter Hilton's Of Angels' Song.
contentParts.summary: Walter Hilton's warning that contemplative union and perceived heavenly song must arise through grace, virtue, humility, and discretion rather than forced imagination or bodily sensation.

contentParts.label: XI. Epistle on the Mixed Life

locators.value: opening supplied from the component beginning f. 57r; exact end folio unresolved

notes: The printed text is composite: Royal supplies the beginning through p. 27 line 29, and the imperfect Lincoln copy supplies the remainder. A separate unshelfmarked Cambridge copy is quoted for dialect comparison.
contentParts.summary: Walter Hilton's pastoral counsel on active, contemplative, and mixed forms of Christian life, teaching the addressee to combine prayer and meditation with obligations to household, dependents, and neighbors.

contentParts.label: XII. An Epistle of Salvation
notes: Perry supplies the title The Virtue of Our Lord's Passion. The current Lincoln catalog uses An Epistle of Salvation; the specialist handlist identifies it as material from Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection.
contentParts.summary: A devotional prose epistle on original and personal sin, salvation through Christ's Passion, penitence, the saving name of Jesus, degrees of charity, and heavenly reward.

contentParts.label: XIII. Two Verse-Prayers to the Virgin Mary
notes: The printed headnote says Harl. MS 1002; current British Library cataloging and DIMEV establish Harley MS 1022.
Neither prayer appears in Perry's 1866 first edition.
contentParts.summary: Two short anonymous Middle English Marian prayers added in the 1921 revision from Harley MS 1022, f. 61v.

contentParts.label: Quene of parage
notes: DIMEV describes two three-line monorhyming stanzas with medial rhyme and rhyme pattern aaa.
contentParts.summary: A six-line orison asking the Virgin, queen of peerage and restored paradise, to teach heavenly bliss, comfort the joyless, and intercede for entry into heaven.

contentParts.label: Mary so milde
contentParts.summary: An eight-line prayer asking Mary, for love of her child, to hear wild petitioners, pour grace on them, build them in bliss, and shield them from sin.

dateStatements.display: By 1349 if the traditional Rolle attributions are accepted; exact composition dates and parts of the canon remain uncertain
notes: The terminus follows Rolle's death and applies only where component authorship is genuine; it is not a date for the later manuscript copies or the edited selection.

dateStatements.display: By Walter Hilton's death in 1396 for Of Angels' Song and the Epistle on the Mixed Life
Fourteenth century if the text is correctly identified as an excerpt from Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection
No later than the c. 1390-c. 1425 Harley MS 1022 witness
notes: This dates the surviving copy, not necessarily composition.

dateStatements.display: Perry's first ten-item edited selection was published in 1866
notes: The 1866 edition lacks revised items VI and XIII and combines revised items III-IV, so it is a prior version rather than the identical thirteen-item unit.

dateStatements.display: Revised, expanded, reset, and reissued in 1921

extent.description: Thirteen top-level numbered sections containing fourteen component items because XIII contains two separate prayers. Items I and III-XII are prose; item II is prose ending in a two-line verse prayer; the two item-XIII prayers total 14 lines in four LG elements. The repository XML has 23 L elements overall: 16 intrinsic lines and seven lines inside editorial quotations or tailnotes in items X and XII. It also has 2 DIV1, 13 DIV2, 9 DIV3, 37 HEAD, 49 PB, 416 MILESTONE, 357 NOTE, 51 P, 1,576 HI, and 86 ABBR elements.

formStatements.description: Eleven numbered items are prose, item II is prose ending in a two-line prayer, and item XIII consists of two verse prayers. Editorial verse quotations inside prose headnotes or tailnotes do not change the cataloged form of those component works.

scope.description: Rubrics, biblical quotations, prayers, formulae, and the first Marian prayer's Latin closer

languageStatements.label: English
notes: Editorial English is not the language of the underlying medieval works.

scope.description: Modern editorial titles, headnotes, marginal explanations, footnotes, and title-page matter retained by the encoding

notes: The title-page phrase 'of Richard Rolle' is retained as the edition-selection title but is not normalized into unscoped sole authorship.
Composition dates, copy dates, edition dates, and digital-encoding dates are kept separate.
The part inventory follows the 1921 revised numbering represented by CME00058, not the ten-item numbering of the 1866 first edition.

next_steps: Consult modern critical editions and authorial-canon studies for each component rather than relying on Perry's 1866 defense.
Preserve manuscript rubrics, Perry's attributions, and current catalog labels as separate assertions.
Keep the catalog summary mixed and scoped until the components are reviewed individually.
openQuestions.question: Which of revised items I-IX belong in the modern Richard Rolle canon, and what confidence should attach to Walter Hilton's authorship of item X and the Hilton source identification for item XII?

next_steps: Collate the Lincoln incipit and explicit against a modern critical edition of the Scale.
Check modern Hilton manuscript scholarship for the title An Epistle of Salvation.
Revise the related-work relation and authorship confidence only after the textual boundary is verified.
openQuestions.question: What exact passage of Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection corresponds to An Epistle of Salvation at Lincoln MS 91, ff. 229v-230v, and is the manuscript unit an excerpt, adaptation, or separately transmitted epistle?

next_steps: Use component critical editions and Linguistic Atlas profiles for each exact witness passage.
Retain the Royal opening's historical Midland diagnosis separately from the dominant Northern witness language.
Do not assign North Yorkshire as the composition place of every selected work merely because Thornton copied them there.
openQuestions.question: Can individual component compositions be dated or localized more precisely without conflating authorial origin, witness dialect, manuscript production, and edition publication?

next_steps: Ask Cambridge University Library to identify Perry's quoted comparison sentence.
Inspect Dd.5.64 at the cited passages or obtain a complete-object reproduction.
Treat the contiguous Notes abbreviations as a high-confidence contextual inference, not as a demonstrated identity with the separate unshelfmarked Mixed Life witness.
openQuestions.question: What is the shelfmark of Perry's unnamed Cambridge Mixed Life witness, and can direct manuscript inspection confirm that Day's contiguous abbreviated 'MS. Camb.' variants are all CUL Dd.5.64?

notes: The selected works and their witnesses are English, but precise places of composition are not assigned from copy location or dialect alone.
This describes witness language, not necessarily the original composition region of every work.

scope.description: The dominant Lincoln Thornton and Harley readings, including the Lincoln remainder of Mixed Life

notes: Perry attributes the Midland diagnosis to Morris's verbal-plural test; the current BL catalog does not independently localize Royal MS 17 C XVIII.

scope.description: Royal MS 17 C XVIII opening supplied through printed p. 27 line 29
Publication place of the 1866 and 1921 edited selections

responsibilities.agentName: Richard Rolle (attributed), Walter Hilton, and anonymous or uncertain authors
responsibilities.display_name: Richard Rolle (attributed), Walter Hilton, and anonymous or uncertain authors

scope.description: Composite catalog display only; component-level responsibilities below carry the actual attribution evidence

assertion.notes: Perry's historical defense of a broad Rolle canon is not treated as settled modern authorship. Component rubrics and current catalog descriptions vary in strength.

scope.description: Texts explicitly or contextually assigned to Richard the Hermit in Thornton, or defended as Rolle's by Perry

assertion.notes: Current institutional and specialist handlists identify the untitled text as Of Angels' Song; Perry instead transmitted Sir Frederic Madden's attribution to Rolle.
The specialist handlist identifies the passage as from Hilton's Scale of Perfection, while the holding-repository catalog titles it An Epistle of Salvation without author attribution.

scope.description: The 1866 first edition and editorial selection retained and revised in 1921
Initial editorial grouping and ordering of the manuscript extracts
The revised and expanded text printed in 1921
The revised glossary and Notes of the immediate 1921 volume; those paratexts are not encoded in CME00058

subjects.label: The name of Jesus, divine love, and contemplative joy
Commandments, virtues, vices, and gifts of the Holy Spirit
Active, contemplative, and mixed forms of Christian life
Christ's Passion, sin, salvation, charity, and heavenly reward
Marian devotion, intercession, grace, and heavenly bliss

summaries.value: A revised editorial selection of thirteen short Middle English devotional texts and prayers associated historically with Richard Rolle. It moves from the name and love of Jesus through temptation, contrition, moral exempla, commandments, gifts of the Holy Spirit, contemplative experience, Walter Hilton's teaching on angels' song and the mixed life, salvation through Christ's Passion, and two anonymous Marian prayers.

I Of the Virtues of the Holy Name; II Hampole's Temptation; III-IV imperfect and perfect contrition; V The Bee and the Stork; VI the enclosed maiden; VII Ten Commandments; VIII Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost; IX delight and yearning for God; X Of Angels' Song; XI Epistle on the Mixed Life; XII An Epistle of Salvation; XIII Quene of parage and Mary so milde.

The record follows the 1921 revised thirteen-item selection encoded by CME00058. It excludes the rest of each physical manuscript, the 1866 preface and Officium, the 1921 prefatory note, Notes, Glossary and Index, advertisements, and other unencoded bound matter, while the lineage manifest records those physical and editorial layers separately.

notes: This is the cataloged edition-selection title. It is not evidence that Rolle authored every component.

work.regionDisplay: England; principally Northern English witnesses, with a Midland opening supplied for the Mixed Life

subjects.label: The name of Jesus, divine love, and contemplative joy
Commandments, virtues, vices, and gifts of the Holy Spirit
Active, contemplative, and mixed forms of Christian life
Christ's Passion, sin, salvation, charity, and heavenly reward
Marian devotion, intercession, grace, and heavenly bliss

work.summary: A revised editorial selection of thirteen short Middle English devotional texts and prayers associated historically with Richard Rolle. It moves from the name and love of Jesus through temptation, contrition, moral exempla, commandments, gifts of the Holy Spirit, contemplative experience, Walter Hilton's teaching on angels' song and the mixed life, salvation through Christ's Passion, and two anonymous Marian prayers.
